title: Town of North Topsail Beach Coastal Barrier Resources System Map Amendment Act of 2025
short_title: Town of North Topsail Beach Coastal Barrier Resources System Map Amendment Act of 2025
plain_summary: This law tells the Secretary of the Interior to remove some land in North Topsail Beach, North Carolina from a special protected coastal area. The town has already decided through its local rules that these pieces of land should not be used for conservation. The Secretary has thirty days to make this change. This means the removed land will no longer have the same federal protections as the rest of the coastal area.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Town of North Topsail Beach Coastal Barrier Resources System Map Amendment Act of two thousand twenty-five.

sections.title: Section 2 Correction to Map
sections.text: Within thirty days after this law is passed, the Secretary of the Interior must fix a government map. The Secretary must remove certain land parcels from a protected area called unit L06 of the John H. Chafee Coastal Barrier Resources System. The land that must be removed includes every piece of property in the town of North Topsail Beach, North Carolina that the town has designated for uses other than conservation through its local zoning rules as of the day this law is passed. The Secretary must treat each removed parcel as meeting the requirements found in section four, part g, paragraph one, subpart B of the Coastal Barrier Resources Act. This law only applies to parts of unit L06 that are inside the town limits of North Topsail Beach. Local zoning ordinance means the zoning rules that were in effect for the town of North Topsail Beach, North Carolina on the day this law was passed. The map that needs to be fixed is part of a set of maps called John H. Chafee Coastal Barrier Resources System that was dated November twenty-fifth, two thousand twenty-four. This specific map shows unit L06 of the John H. Chafee Coastal Barrier Resources System.
